sessions.title: Team Awareness Reality Check
sessions.principle:
Every athlete affects the team environment whether they intend to or not.
Your behavior, effort, tone, and focus shape the room.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, every athlete protects the room.
Culture grows from daily behavior.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: Answer honestly: What behaviors strengthen a team? What behaviors weaken it? Which one do you contribute most right now?
questions: What behaviors strengthen a team immediately?
What behaviors weaken a room fast?
How do effort, tone, and focus affect others?
What are you contributing most right now?
What must change first?
sessions.deliverable: 3 team strengths + 3 team weaknesses + self assessment

scripture.text: Each of you should look not only to your own interests.

sessions.title: Energy in the Room
sessions.principle:
Athletes bring energy that spreads through the team.
Negative attitudes weaken rooms quickly. Disciplined energy lifts standards.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, we bring working energy.
Energy spreads faster than instruction.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: Describe the energy you usually bring to the room and how it affects teammates.
questions: What kind of energy do you usually bring?
How does that energy affect teammates?
When does your energy drop?
What kind of energy strengthens a room?

scripture.text: Encourage one another and build each other up.

sessions.title: Respect Across the Team
sessions.principle:
Team respect means valuing every athlete in the room.
Strong rooms treat beginners, veterans, winners, and strugglers with the same baseline respect.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, respect applies to everyone.
Respect stabilizes teams.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: Why does team respect matter even when athletes have different skill levels?
questions: Why does respect matter across all skill levels?
What happens when respect becomes selective?
How should stronger athletes treat weaker ones?
How should struggling athletes still carry themselves?

scripture.text: Show proper respect to everyone.

sessions.title: Your Role in the Room
sessions.principle:
Every athlete has influence inside the team.
Some athletes lift standards. Others quietly lower them.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, everyone carries responsibility.
Influence is built by repetition.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: Describe the role you currently play in the room and the role you should aim to play.
questions: What role do you play right now?
How do others likely experience you?
Does your current role raise or lower standards?
What role should you aim to become?

scripture.text: Let your light shine before others.

sessions.title: Protecting Team Culture
sessions.principle:
Strong teams protect their standards.
If athletes allow laziness, disrespect, or recklessness, culture weakens quickly.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, we protect our culture.
Culture survives through protection.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: What behaviors must be protected to keep a strong team culture?
questions: What standards define a strong room?
What behaviors weaken culture fastest?
Why does culture need protection?
What should athletes defend immediately?

scripture.text: Stand firm in the faith.

sessions.title: Team Accountability
sessions.principle:
Teams improve when athletes hold each other accountable.
Ignoring problems does not help teammates grow.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, we hold each other accountable.
Accountability protects standards.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: Why is accountability important for team growth?
questions: Why does accountability matter?
What happens when teammates ignore problems?
How can accountability be respectful?
What should never be left unaddressed?

scripture.text: Better is open rebuke than hidden love.

sessions.title: Encouraging Teammates
sessions.principle:
Encouragement strengthens effort and resilience.
Athletes who support teammates during hard training build stronger teams.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, we encourage effort.
Encouragement multiplies effort.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: Describe a moment when encouragement improved a teammate’s effort.
questions: What kind of encouragement helps most?
When does encouragement matter most?
How can your words affect effort?
What kind of support strengthens resilience?

scripture.text: Therefore encourage one another.

sessions.title: Shared Responsibility
sessions.principle:
Teams function when responsibility is shared.
Every athlete contributes to safety, focus, and culture.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, everyone contributes.
Strong teams share responsibility.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: What responsibilities should every athlete carry inside the team?
questions: What responsibilities belong to every athlete?
Why can’t responsibility stay with coaches alone?
How does shared responsibility strengthen teams?
What responsibility do you need to carry better?

scripture.text: Two are better than one.

sessions.title: Team Communication
sessions.principle:
Clear communication keeps teams aligned.
Teams work better when athletes communicate honestly and respectfully.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, we communicate clearly.
Communication builds trust.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: Why does communication matter inside teams?
questions: Why does communication matter in a team room?
What happens when athletes stop communicating?
What makes communication respectful?
How does honest communication build alignment?

scripture.text: Let your conversation be always full of grace.

sessions.title: Trust Across the Team
sessions.principle:
Teams grow stronger when trust expands beyond one partner.
Trust must exist across the entire room for a team to thrive.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, trust extends across the team.
Trust grows through reliable behavior.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: How do athletes earn trust from the entire team?
questions: What behaviors build teamwide trust?
How is team trust different from one-partner trust?
What weakens trust across the room?
What do reliable athletes do consistently?

scripture.text: Whoever walks in integrity walks securely.

sessions.title: Representing the Team
sessions.principle:
Athletes represent their team both inside and outside training.
Your behavior reflects on the entire group.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, we represent the team with honor.
Representation shapes reputation.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: How should athletes represent their team through behavior?
questions: What does representing the team well look like?
How does behavior reflect on others?
What damages team reputation fastest?
What standard should guide you in public and private?

scripture.text: Conduct yourselves in a manner worthy.

sessions.title: Supporting Teammates in Difficulty
sessions.principle:
Teams grow stronger when athletes support struggling teammates.
Support during difficult moments builds unity.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, we support each other.
Support strengthens unity.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: Describe how teammates should respond when someone struggles.
questions: What does real support look like?
What does fake support look like?
How can athletes strengthen a struggling teammate?
Why does support build unity?

scripture.text: Carry each other’s burdens.

sessions.title: Handling Team Conflict
sessions.principle:
Conflict handled well can strengthen teams.
Teams do not avoid conflict. They handle it with maturity and respect.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, we handle conflict with discipline.
Discipline keeps conflict productive.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: How should teammates resolve disagreements respectfully?
questions: Why do teams need a mature way to handle conflict?
What makes conflict destructive?
What makes conflict productive?
How should athletes speak during disagreement?

scripture.text: Blessed are the peacemakers.

sessions.title: Shared Standards
sessions.principle:
Teams succeed when everyone follows shared standards.
When standards slip, the entire team feels it.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, we follow shared standards.
Shared standards create strong teams.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: What standards must every athlete follow to keep the team strong?
questions: What standards should every athlete follow?
What happens when standards become optional?
How do shared standards protect discipline?
Which standard needs the most reinforcement right now?

scripture.text: Let all things be done decently and in order.

sessions.title: Team Reliability
sessions.principle:
Reliable teammates build strong teams.
When teammates know they can rely on you, trust grows.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, teammates are reliable.
Reliability builds trust.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: What behaviors make someone reliable for the team?
questions: What makes an athlete reliable?
What makes someone unreliable?
How does reliability affect trust?
Where do you need to become more dependable?

scripture.text: Many claim to have unfailing love, but a faithful person who can find?

sessions.title: Team Trust Retest
sessions.principle:
Trust grows through repeated responsible behavior.
Teams grow when athletes continue strengthening trust through discipline.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, trust grows daily.
Trust grows through consistent behavior.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: List 3 habits that strengthen trust within the team and 2 habits that weaken it.
questions: What habits strengthen trust across the team?
What habits weaken it quickly?
Which habit matters most for you?
What should change immediately?

scripture.text: Whoever walks in integrity walks securely.

sessions.title: Do Not Be the Weak Link
sessions.principle:
Teams suffer when one athlete consistently drags effort down.
Low effort spreads just like high effort.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, we refuse to be the weak link.
Weak links weaken everyone.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: What behaviors make someone the weak link on a team?
questions: What behaviors weaken the team most?
How does low effort spread?
Why is being the weak link dangerous to culture?
What do you need to fix first?

scripture.text: Carry your own load.

sessions.title: Reliable Effort for the Team
sessions.principle:
Reliable effort stabilizes team culture.
When teammates know you will work consistently, trust grows and standards rise.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, effort is reliable.
Consistency creates trust.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: Describe what reliable effort looks like on a team.
questions: What does reliable effort look like daily?
How does it affect trust?
What makes effort inconsistent?
What standard should teammates be able to count on?

scripture.text: Let us not grow weary of doing good.

sessions.title: Showing Up Prepared
sessions.principle:
Preparation respects the team.
Late arrivals, missing gear, and careless preparation slow the entire group.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, we show up ready.
Preparation shows respect before work begins.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: What preparation habits show respect for your team?
questions: What does readiness look like before practice starts?
What habits weaken team readiness?
How does poor preparation affect others?
What one habit must improve now?

scripture.text: Prepare your work outside.

sessions.title: Shared Workload
sessions.principle:
Teams grow when workload is shared fairly.
Some athletes carry more work while others drift.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, we share the work.
Shared work builds shared strength.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: How should teammates share responsibility for effort?
questions: What does fair workload look like?
What happens when some athletes drift?
How does shared work build trust?
What responsibility do you carry best? Worst?

scripture.text: Two are better than one.

sessions.title: Team Energy
sessions.principle:
Your attitude affects the whole team.
Complaining, laziness, and negativity weaken team momentum.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, we bring working energy.
Energy spreads faster than effort.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: How does negative energy spread across a team?
questions: How does negative energy spread?
What does it do to effort?
What kind of energy lifts a team?
What do you usually bring in?

scripture.text: Encourage one another.

sessions.title: Handling Frustration on a Team
sessions.principle:
Frustration must not poison team work.
Emotion is normal, but undisciplined frustration spreads quickly.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, frustration stays controlled.
Control protects the team.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: How should athletes manage frustration during team training?
questions: What does frustration do to team culture?
How does it spread?
What does mature control look like?
What should happen before frustration spills onto others?

scripture.text: Better a patient person than a warrior.

sessions.title: Shared Success
sessions.principle:
Team victories belong to everyone who contributes.
Strong teams celebrate improvement together, not just individual wins.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, success is shared.
Shared victories strengthen teams.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: Why should athletes value team success over personal credit?
questions: Why is shared success important?
What happens when athletes chase only personal credit?
How does shared success strengthen a room?
What mindset helps athletes celebrate together?

scripture.text: Rejoice with those who rejoice.

sessions.title: Team Standard
sessions.principle:
Teams need a clear effort standard.
Without shared expectations, discipline collapses.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, we hold a clear team standard.
Standards create discipline.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: Write the effort standard your team should live by.
questions: What should your team’s effort standard be?
Why must it be shared?
What weakens it most?
How should teammates reinforce it?

scripture.text: Be steadfast in your work.

sessions.title: Protecting Teammates
sessions.principle:
Teams protect their members physically and mentally.
Athletes must watch out for each other’s safety and growth.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, we protect teammates.
Protection builds trust.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: What behaviors protect teammates during training?
questions: What behaviors protect teammates physically?
What behaviors protect them mentally?
How does protection build trust?
What should athletes notice sooner?

scripture.text: Carry each other’s burdens.

sessions.title: Team Accountability
sessions.principle:
Teammates help each other maintain standards.
Accountability keeps teams disciplined.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, teammates hold each other accountable.
Accountability protects culture.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: How should teammates hold each other accountable respectfully?
questions: Why does accountability protect culture?
How can correction stay respectful?
What happens when teammates ignore standards?
What should accountability sound like?

scripture.text: Better open rebuke than hidden love.

sessions.title: Team Loyalty
sessions.principle:
Loyalty means supporting teammates through difficulty.
Teams break when loyalty disappears.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, we stay loyal to the team.
Loyalty builds unity.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: What does loyalty look like inside a team?
questions: What does loyalty look like in action?
What weakens loyalty?
How does loyalty build unity?
When is loyalty most needed?

scripture.text: A friend loves at all times.

sessions.title: Helping New Teammates
sessions.principle:
Strong teams help new athletes integrate safely.
Beginners learn culture from experienced teammates.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, we help newcomers.
Mentorship grows teams.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: How should teams treat new members?
questions: How should experienced athletes treat new teammates?
What helps beginners feel safe and clear?
How does mentorship shape culture?
What should never happen to a new athlete?

scripture.text: Teach others also.

sessions.title: Protecting the Room
sessions.principle:
Athletes share responsibility for protecting team culture.
When standards slip, strong athletes step up.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, we protect the room.
Culture survives through protection.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: What behaviors damage a team environment?
questions: What behaviors damage the room most?
Why do standards need protecting?
What does stepping up look like?
What should never be ignored?

scripture.text: Stand firm in the faith.

sessions.title: Correcting Teammates
sessions.principle:
Correction should strengthen teammates, not humiliate them.
Strong teams correct behavior respectfully.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, correction builds teammates.
Respectful correction strengthens teams.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: How should teammates correct each other respectfully?
questions: What makes correction helpful?
What makes it humiliating?
How should mature correction sound?
When should correction happen right away?

scripture.text: Speak the truth in love.

sessions.title: Shared Safety
sessions.principle:
Safety is everyone’s responsibility.
Teams train harder when everyone protects safety.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, safety is shared.
Safety builds trust.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: What responsibilities keep team training safe?
questions: What responsibilities belong to everyone?
How does shared safety build trust?
What weakens team safety fastest?
What should athletes notice earlier?

scripture.text: The prudent see danger and take refuge.

sessions.title: Respecting Every Teammate
sessions.principle:
Respect must apply to every teammate.
Selective respect damages unity.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, respect is universal.
Respect unifies teams.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: Why must respect apply to every teammate?
questions: Why does selective respect damage unity?
How should respect look across differences?
What behaviors expose fake respect?
What does universal respect look like in practice?

scripture.text: Show proper respect to everyone.

sessions.title: Raising the Team Level
sessions.principle:
Strong athletes raise the standard for everyone.
One disciplined athlete can lift the entire room.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, we raise the level.
Leadership often begins with effort.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: How can an athlete raise the effort level of teammates?
questions: How does one athlete raise the room?
What habits raise team effort?
Why does leadership often begin with example?
What can you do this week to raise the level?

scripture.text: Set an example for the believers.

sessions.title: Team Standards Under Pressure
sessions.principle:
Pressure reveals team culture.
Stress and fatigue test discipline.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, pressure does not break standards.
Pressure reveals preparation.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: What happens to teams when pressure increases?
questions: What breaks first under pressure?
What survives if culture is strong?
How does preparation show under stress?
What standard must hold no matter what?

scripture.text: Be strong and courageous.

sessions.title: Shared Victory
sessions.principle:
Team victories belong to everyone.
Success grows when athletes celebrate together.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, victory is shared.
Shared victory strengthens teams.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: Why should athletes celebrate team success together?
questions: Why does shared victory matter?
What happens when athletes only value personal credit?
How does celebrating together strengthen a team?
What mindset helps athletes share success well?

scripture.text: Rejoice with those who rejoice.

sessions.title: Team Character Under Loss
sessions.principle:
Loss reveals true team character.
How a team reacts to defeat matters more than the loss itself.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, we respond to loss with discipline.
Loss can build stronger teams.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: How should teams respond after losing?
questions: What does loss usually expose?
What response weakens a team after defeat?
What response strengthens it?
How can loss build something better?

scripture.text: The righteous fall seven times and rise again.

sessions.title: Trust in Competition
sessions.principle:
Competition requires deep team trust.
Athletes perform better when they trust teammates behind them.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, teammates trust each other.
Trust strengthens performance.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: Why is trust important during competition?
questions: How does trust affect performance?
What breaks trust during competition?
What support should teammates feel behind them?
What makes competitive trust strong?

scripture.text: Whoever walks in integrity walks securely.

sessions.title: Team Reputation
sessions.principle:
Teams earn reputations through consistent behavior.
People quickly learn what kind of team you are.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, reputation is earned through honor.
Reputation reflects culture.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: What reputation should your team have?
questions: What reputation does your team currently project?
What reputation should it earn?
What behaviors build that reputation?
What habits damage it?

scripture.text: A good name is more desirable than great riches.

sessions.title: What Makes a Strong Team?
sessions.principle:
Strong teams combine discipline, trust, and shared effort.
Great teams are built on habits repeated daily.
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, strong teams are built intentionally.
Strong teams are built through disciplined habits.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: Define what makes a strong team using behaviors, not feelings.
questions: What habits make teams strong?
What behaviors prove team strength?
What weak habits must disappear?
What would someone else see in a truly strong team?

scripture.text: Let all things be done decently and in order.

sessions.title: Retest: Team Awareness
sessions.principle:
Team awareness should grow with discipline.
This session returns to the original question: how do you affect the team?
sessions.houseStandard:
In this house, we measure growth honestly.
Team awareness grows through honest reflection.
Say it out loud. Twice.
sessions.prompt: Repeat Session 1 questions and compare your answers.
questions: What changed most in how you affect the team?
What still weakens your team impact?
What standard improved most?
What is your next commitment to the room?

scripture.text: Examine yourselves.
